senses.definition: A senior or first among presbyters in certain Christian churches; a high-ranking elder or priest.
examples: The council appointed the protopresbyter to oversee several parishes.
As protopresbyter, he chaired the regional church meeting.

senses.definition: The first or leading body of presbyters within a church hierarchy; a primary group of elders.
examples: The protopresbytery supervises the administrative work of the region.
Meetings of the protopresbytery are held quarterly.

senses.definition: In geometry, an early or primitive form of prism; a prism-like geometric construct in higher-dimensional contexts.
examples: The architect described the protoprism as a building block in the model.
Mathematicians study how protoprisms relate to other polytopes.

senses.definition: An early or proto form of proteose, a class of protein digestion products in biochemistry.
examples: Researchers isolated a protoproteose during milk digestion studies.
The protoproteose fraction was analyzed for activity.

senses.definition: An original or early form of Protestant; a proto-Protestant thinker or movement.
examples: The text discusses protoprotestant influences on later reform movements.
Historians debate the role of protoprotestants in late medieval religious debates.

senses.definition: An early or primitive member of the group Protopterus; a lungfish or its early relatives in paleontological contexts.
examples: Fossils of protopteran forms help scientists trace lungfish evolution.
The protopteran lineage shows adaptation to freshwater environments.

senses.definition: A biological family name used in taxonomy for a group of ancient lungfishes.
examples: Fossils from the Protopteridae family illustrate early aquatic vertebrates.
Researchers classify the new specimen within Protopteridae.

senses.definition: An ancestral plant lineage; a primitive leafy plant related to ferns and lycophytes.
examples: Paleobotanists study protopteridophytes to understand early plant evolution.
Fossils show protopteridophytes with simple vascular systems.

senses.definition: Having or capable of forming a pectoral fin or adapted to swimming in aquatic environments; the term can describe early lungfish anatomy.
examples: The protopterous organism lived in shallow waters.
Fossil specimens show protopterous features.

senses.definition: A genus of lungfishes; the scientific name for a group of living fossil fish.
examples: The genus Protopterus includes several freshwater species.
Researchers collected Protopterus specimens for study.

senses.definition: A structure or shape resembling a primitive pyramid; a term used in geology or archaeology for early pyramidal forms.
examples: The artifact displayed a protopyramid carving.
Geologists compared the protopyramid with later pyramid structures.

senses.definition: An early or primitive root or original form of something, especially in scientific terms.
examples: The protore of the enzyme was identified in early experiments.
Chemists labeled the molecule's protore as a starting point.

senses.definition: A person who is an early member or supporter of a rebel movement; a proto-rebel.
examples: The protorebel spoke at the underground rally.
Historians debate the motives of protorebels in the region.

senses.definition: Relating to early forms of religious belief or practice; preceding established religious systems.
examples: Protoreligious ideas influenced later spiritual movements.
Archaeologists study protoreligious sites to understand early worship.

senses.definition: Relating to the early or primitive reptile-like lineage; used in paleontology.
examples: Protoreptilian fossils reveal ancient reptile characteristics.
Scientists propose protoreptilian traits as ancestral features.

senses.definition: In biology or chemistry, a primitive or initial form of a root or chemical group labeled as 'rest' or 'res' in some nomenclatures.
examples: Researchers describe the protores as an early scaffold in the pathway.
The term protores appears in the schematic diagrams.

senses.definition: An extinct early horse-like mammal; a primitive member of the horse family (Equidae).
examples: Fossils of protorohippus help illustrate horse evolution.
The protorohippus fossils show early tooth structure.

senses.definition: An extinct reptile-like animal from the superorder Archosauromorpha; early prehistoric reptiles.
examples: Protorosaurs roamed ancient landscapes long before dinosaurs.
Fossil beds contain several protorosaur specimens.

senses.definition: A clade or grouping within archosauromorph reptiles that includes protorosaurs.
examples: Protorosauria is studied to understand early archosauromorph evolution.
Fossil evidence places certain species within Protorosauria.

senses.definition: A member of the Protorosauria or related group; an animal within that clade.
examples: Researchers identified a protorosaurian specimen.
Prot summaries describe protorosaurian features.

senses.definition: A family name within Protorosauria; a taxonomic group of early reptile-like animals.
examples: Protorosauridae includes several primitive archosauromorphs.
Taxonomic references list Protorosauridae as a family.

senses.definition: A taxonomic category of early archosauromorph reptiles; an ancestor group within Protorosauria.
examples: Fossil analysis places the species as a protorosauroid.
Protorosauroids provide clues to reptile evolution.

senses.definition: An extinct genus of early reptile; one of the first known archosauromorphs.
examples: Protorosaurus specimens are key to understanding early archosauromorphs.
The Protorosaurus genus appears in early Triassic rocks.

senses.definition: An extinct order of insects considered primitive relative to true Orthoptera (grasshoppers and crickets).
examples: Protorthoptera fossils illuminate early insect evolution.
The order Protorthoptera includes several primitive forms.

senses.definition: A member of Protorthoptera or a primitive orthopteran; an early grasshopper-like insect.
examples: Protorthopteran wings show early flight adaptation.
Researchers describe a protorthopteran specimen from the site.
